113057.explanation: Kernachtig Druten supports equal treatment and equal opportunities. For responsible positions, suitability should come first - with equal suitability also reflecting the population equally.

113059.explanation: Self-reliance, shared responsibility, and engagement from society are valuable. They also foster connection. Let's tackle this together!

113062.explanation: Prefer local initiative and saving/earning locally rather than enriching anonymous entrepreneurs far away.

113065.explanation: The housing policy that Kernachtig Druten ensured in 2025 provides room for this. Preferably housing on the employer's own grounds, rather than buying up homes in neighborhoods for 'short stay' migrant workers.

113069.explanation: Although consumer fireworks are banned, the new law allows, for example, (neighborhood) associations and other organizations to organize fireworks shows. We do not see it as the municipality's role to organize events itself; rather to facilitate them.

113073.explanation: Housing construction can take place in locations determined in the Environmental Vision. This was recently drawn up partly at the request of Kernachtig Druten.

113076.explanation: As far as Kernachtig Druten is concerned, educational housing is one of the most important themes in the coming 4 years. A new location must be built for Pax Christi and also (re)construction of 't Klòsterhûfke and the Octopus. The Kubus also needs a new gymnasium.

113080.explanation: This is already the situation in the municipality of Druten. We consider the library a crucial facility for, for example, language development.

113081.explanation: We must enforce much more firmly on waste dumping, nuisance, speed, dog fouling, illegal parking, drugs, and fireworks.

113083.explanation: We believe the municipality of Druten must take responsibility regarding domestic violence, women's shelters, and other forms of protected or safe housing. Much of this is already arranged regionally.

113084.explanation: Both from caring for our residents and from preserving social cohesion, it is important that people have a chance for housing in their own environment. Kernachtig Druten has repeatedly advocated this.

113086.explanation: Highly dependent on specific circumstances per location. Where possible, the municipality should not stand in the way.

113087.explanation: New construction outside the existing village boundaries is only desired in locations designated by the municipality. Not the project developer, but the municipality decides where building takes place! Where we want to build in the coming years has recently been recorded in the Environmental Vision.

113089.explanation: This is an important safety net in poverty policy. Previously, Kernachtig Druten took the initiative to establish and fund an anti-poverty fund.

113090.explanation: We stand for the 'working poor,' people who fall outside various schemes and aid but still cannot make ends meet.

113092.explanation: Realizing more charging points is welcome, but an initiative for the free market and local entrepreneurs.

113093.explanation: Kernachtig Druten wants to address various traffic situations to increase safety and flow. Many of these locations already have a 30 km/h speed limit. So, other measures are needed!

113094.explanation: People have freedom of choice in selecting a healthcare provider.

113097.explanation: We do not want the municipality to profit from dog taxes. The tax is based solely on the costs of proper facilities such as trash bins, poop bags, off-leash parks, and enforcement. Therefore, this tax can be reduced.

113098.explanation: This was only recently introduced and has a level that fits well regionally.

113099.explanation: Until 2030, there is a need for 5.5 hectares of new industrial sites. This was recently studied and recorded, among others, in the Environmental Vision. More industrial sites are not needed for the time being.

113101.explanation: We follow national policies and regulations on this.

113102.explanation: In combating waste dumping and ensuring a clean, livable public space, this can be one of the links. Our recycling center already has free waste streams.

113106.explanation: Our dialect is beautiful but not a language. We consider it more important that children learn Dutch.

113108.explanation: The preferred building height in the core of Druten is two stories (with a roof). Only exceptionally can deviations be made in distinctive, urban-planned locations.

113110.explanation: In new developments, we find it important that a customized parking standard applies. One development might lead to more parking spaces; another to fewer. This depends on the location and target group.

113112.explanation: Kernachtig Druten is a strong opponent of wind turbines. The only party so firm and consistent. Wind turbines over 200 meters tall cause health complaints, disrupt the landscape, and kill biodiversity. And Druten already contributes more than enough to the energy transition.

113115.explanation: Safety and traffic flow benefit more from other measures. We also do not want to further increase the attracting effect of the N322 to more traffic.

113116.explanation: At Kernachtig Druten's initiative, several locations in our municipality were changed and designated as 'Ecologically valuable areas' in the Environmental Vision. These include, among others, the brick pits at Heersweg / Kerkweg.

113057.explanation: We find diversity in leadership important and strive for a good balance. Quality and experience remain important criteria, not the quantity of the ratio.

113059.explanation: In other municipalities we see good examples but also risks. It can work if residents are well supported and responsibilities are clear. We assess this per situation, not as a standard solution.

113062.explanation: We tackle the energy transition together with residents and entrepreneurs. Financial support from the national government and the province is indispensable.

113065.explanation: Employers are responsible for good housing for seasonal workers, but this does not necessarily have to be on their own premises. We think along about appropriate solutions.

113069.explanation: We see no role for the municipality in organizing fireworks shows. Private initiatives in association contexts are supported, within national legislation.

113073.explanation: Living on agricultural land is possible in some cases, but only carefully and appropriate to the area. Requests from farmers are treated the same as those from other residents.

113076.explanation: Every village deserves a good primary school, but addressing everything within one council period is not realistic. We work according to the integral housing plan and provide temporary solutions where needed.

113080.explanation: We find the current situation with a central library in Druten appropriate and sufficient.

113081.explanation: Visible enforcement contributes to safety and trust in the neighborhood.

113083.explanation: Help for victims is essential. This requires good regional cooperation.

113084.explanation: We primarily build for our own residents, within existing legal possibilities.

113086.explanation: Holiday homes can (temporarily) help alleviate the housing shortage. This does require clear rules and a careful policy framework.

113087.explanation: We prefer building within the villages but do not rule out expansion in advance. The character of the village centers is leading; villages should not grow into one another.

113089.explanation: Waiving fees is not always the best solution. We prefer targeted support and tailored approaches to address real problems.

113090.explanation: Raising thresholds can actually create new bottlenecks. We want to prevent people from falling through the cracks.

113092.explanation: The transition to electric driving requires sufficient charging points in neighborhoods.

113093.explanation: 30 km/h makes residential areas safer and more pleasant to live in.

113094.explanation: We value appropriate care, but due to the pressing capacity of existing general practitioner practices, the priority now is to keep good primary care accessible for everyone.

113097.explanation: There are several dog facilities in Druten. The costs for these must be covered. As a party, we also find it desirable that the number of dog waste bins increases.

113098.explanation: We want to make Druten more attractive to visitors. In the future, an increase may fit with a broader offer, but not now.

113099.explanation: Business parks may grow carefully with the scale of our municipality. This provides local employment, strengthens the economic structure, and keeps facilities and knowledge in Druten.

113101.explanation: We strive for a fair distribution, considering financial capacity and local home seekers, in line with national legislation. So not necessarily giving priority.

113102.explanation: This is partly already arranged in Druten. Recyclable waste can continue to be delivered free of charge; there is a fee for bulky waste.

113106.explanation: It is up to education itself to decide whether attention to the regional dialect fits within the curriculum. We prefer extra attention to healthy nutrition, digitalization, citizenship, and regional cultural heritage.

113108.explanation: New construction must fit the village scale and environment.

113110.explanation: This is not standardly needed everywhere. Parking needs vary by neighborhood. Tailored solutions are necessary, with extra attention to seniors and people with disabilities.

113112.explanation: We are not in favor of wind turbines, but if they are needed, the municipality will decide where they are placed. We do this by establishing preferred and exclusion zones based on clear criteria, such as distance to residential centers.

113115.explanation: Together with the province of Gelderland, a study was conducted on the traffic situation on the N322. Doubling the carriageway toward Ewijk appears to be the best solution for safety and accessibility. Funds are reserved for this, but additional lobbying remains necessary.

113116.explanation: Where opportunities exist for nature development, we support it. It must fit with the environment and the use of the area.

113057.explanation: Disagree, because the composition of the executive board should be based on quality, experience, and administrative suitability. A fixed distribution based on gender can lead to the most suitable candidates not being selected.

113059.explanation: Agree, because residents often know better what is needed in their neighborhood. Self-management increases involvement and responsibility, can save costs, and ensures facilities are better maintained and used.

113062.explanation: Agree, because local energy initiatives contribute to the energy transition. Municipal support lowers the threshold for residents, stimulates collaboration, and ensures sustainable energy is produced and used locally.

113065.explanation: Agree, because employers thus take responsibility for good and controlled housing. This prevents nuisance in residential areas and ensures better living conditions for seasonal workers close to their work.

113069.explanation: Disagree, because a fireworks show involves high costs. That money can also be spent on care, youth, or sustainability. In addition, there are concerns about the environment, animal welfare, and noise nuisance.

113073.explanation: Disagree, because building on agricultural land accelerates urbanization of the countryside. This harms the landscape and nature and may put the agricultural function under pressure, while housing construction can better take place at designated locations.

113076.explanation: Agree, because good and safe school buildings are essential for quality education. Investing in maintenance, sustainability, and indoor climate contributes to better learning outcomes and a healthy environment for students and staff.

113080.explanation: Disagree, because libraries in the villages must remain accessible and close by. Small libraries enhance livability and social cohesion in the centers; centralization reduces accessibility, especially for elderly and young people.

113081.explanation: Agree, because more community enforcement officers provide visible enforcement and a safer feeling in the neighborhoods. Their presence prevents nuisance, increases compliance with rules, and contributes to a livable and safe municipality.

113083.explanation: Because shelter for victims of domestic violence is already well organized regionally with contributions from all municipalities, including Druten. An additional facility in Druten itself is therefore not necessary as long as the current cooperation functions well.

113084.explanation: Agree, because priority for local home seekers ensures residents can continue to live in their own municipality. This strengthens social cohesion and prevents people with local ties from missing out.

113086.explanation: This hardly occurs in Druten. If it does, each situation can be reviewed to see whether permanent residence is desirable and appropriate, so that tailor-made solutions remain possible without unwanted consequences.

113087.explanation: Agree, because building outside the village boundaries damages the landscape and puts the village character under pressure. Building within the existing cores keeps Druten compact, livable, and recognizable for residents.

113089.explanation: Disagree, because waiving fees prevents residents with low incomes from getting into further financial trouble. It provides livelihood security and prevents debts, while the municipality retains attention to social justice.

113090.explanation: Disagree, because help must remain targeted at people who truly need it. Raising the income limit dilutes support and causes limited resources to reach residents in greatest financial need less effectively.

113092.explanation: Agree, because extra charging points encourage the switch to electric driving. By placing these in the right locations and in consultation with residents, parking pressure remains manageable and there is support in the neighborhood.

113093.explanation: Agree, because 30 km/h increases traffic safety and reduces the chance of severe accidents. It creates calmer traffic and a more livable residential environment for pedestrians and cyclists.

113094.explanation: Disagree, because care must primarily be qualitative, available, and affordable. Always having a choice based on religion or culture is not feasible and can put pressure on the organization and accessibility of care.

113097.explanation: Agree, because dog owners already contribute through general taxes. Abolishing this is fairer and prevents additional financial burdens, while responsible dog ownership can also be encouraged without a specific tax.

113098.explanation: Disagree, because tourist tax in Druten yields hardly any revenue. An increase brings little benefit and can reduce attractiveness for visitors, without clear financial gain.

113099.explanation: Agree, because extra business parks provide space for local entrepreneurs and attract new companies. This creates employment, economic growth, and strengthens the municipality's financial base.

113101.explanation: Disagree, because scarce social rental homes should first be available to regular home seekers with local ties. Priority for status holders increases pressure on the housing market and can be perceived as unfair.

113102.explanation: Agree, because free disposal at the recycling center prevents illegal dumping. It lowers the threshold to separate waste neatly and contributes to a clean and sustainable municipality.

113106.explanation: Agree, because attention to the Maas and Waals dialect contributes to the preservation of local culture and identity. It increases children's involvement with their environment and keeps the dialect alive for future generations.

113108.explanation: Disagree, because high-rise residential buildings do not fit the village character of Druten. They disrupt the streetscape and scale of the centers, while housing construction can better align with the existing, small-scale buildings.

113110.explanation: Agree, because additional parking spaces reduce parking pressure in residential areas. This prevents nuisance and unsafe situations and ensures a more accessible and livable residential environment for residents.

113112.explanation: Agree, because wind turbines affect the landscape and village character of Druten. Moreover, Druten already sufficiently invests in alternative forms of sustainable energy generation, making wind turbines unnecessary.

113115.explanation: Agree, because widening the N322 improves traffic flow. This increases road safety and accessibility of Druten and the region, which is important for residents and entrepreneurs.

113116.explanation: Disagree, because additional protected nature areas further limit space for housing, agriculture, and development. Druten already has valuable nature; the focus should be on good management of existing areas rather than expansion.

113057.explanation: We strive for as equal a representation of men and women in the executive board as possible, but it is not an end goal in itself.

113059.explanation: This is part of the right to challenge. Good agreements must be made between municipalities and residents.

113062.explanation: If there are local initiatives from residents that substantially contribute to solving the energy problem, the municipality should cooperate but not provide financial support.

113065.explanation: Preferably on private property, or at another suitable residential location.

113069.explanation: There is a great demand for fireworks at New Year's Eve. With the upcoming national ban, not everyone may set off fireworks anymore, so the municipality can collaborate with residents to provide alternatives.

113073.explanation: This is already possible under conditions, for example if you demolish barns.

113076.explanation: This is not necessary for some school buildings. The comprehensive housing plan currently being drafted will indicate when which buildings are due. It is clear that some buildings are outdated and need renovation or replacement.

113080.explanation: That is already the case, but it is important to maintain the library drop-off point in the village centers.

113081.explanation: This has already been implemented; safety is also a task of the police.

113083.explanation: A women's shelter can be organized regionally for initial acute shelter, but it is important that women can register in the municipality and that upon rehousing, the woman (if she wishes) can return to her own network within the municipality.

113084.explanation: A significant portion of the housing must be available to home-seekers from the municipality. Additionally, some housing can be reserved for special target groups or for example through lottery.

113086.explanation: This conflicts with zoning and environmental plans, and homes are not designed for it. It is not applicable in the municipality of Druten.

113087.explanation: Only when there is no longer any possibility for infill development should the municipality consider locations outside the village boundaries.

113090.explanation: The current limits are good because there are many additional measures to help people with financial problems, allowing for tailor-made solutions (e.g., Leergeld foundation, adult fund, exceptional assistance, urgent needs foundation, appliance scheme, supplementary health insurance).

113092.explanation: This encourages electric driving, but only if there is demand, meaning no overload on the already available charging points. People who occupy charging points for too long must be discouraged.

113093.explanation: This is not feasible everywhere and therefore cannot be enforced. If desired, this can be indicated for example by zones.

113094.explanation: PGB offers the possibility for personal choice, but then only reliable or contracted providers. This helps maintain quality.

113097.explanation: Dog poo is often the number one annoyance for residents. Dog owners must pay for the nuisance they cause. Funds from the dog tax should be used to provide facilities for dog owners, such as dog exercise fields or waste bins.

113098.explanation: The municipality’s rate is about €0.50 lower than the national average but equal to comparable municipalities around Druten. The tourist tax may be raised slightly to the national average.

113099.explanation: At this moment, the supply meets the demand, so no. If demand increases or existing industrial sites are full, new industrial sites may be necessary.

113101.explanation: Up to a maximum of 10% of the rental homes; this is a continuation of existing policy.

113102.explanation: This is already free for most waste streams.

113106.explanation: If there is demand from schools and students, the municipality should assist in keeping cultural-historical heritage intact.

113108.explanation: Where appropriate, high-rise buildings may be allowed. The municipality must guide this and determine suitable locations for high-rise development (High-rise vision). This makes it easier to realize affordable apartments.

113110.explanation: A quality residential environment also requires space for playing, greenery, and slow traffic. If all space is used for parking, these aspects are compromised.

113112.explanation: We see no suitable locations in the municipality where a safe distance (4 times tip height) to homes can be achieved without seriously damaging the landscape.

113115.explanation: It is important to improve traffic flow and safety. Widening attracts additional cut-through traffic, making it busier with drivers who don’t need to be there.

113116.explanation: Agree, if it can be combined with recreational functions.

113057.explanation: The right person in the right place is more important than gender.

113059.explanation: Citizen participation in their environment is very important.

113062.explanation: Sustainable energy generation must pay for itself. Much support for energy generation is already provided at the national level. Municipalities have no financial capacity for this.

113065.explanation: In housing migrant workers in our residential neighborhoods, we see too many people in one home. This causes nuisance for neighbors. Furthermore, these people do not integrate into the neighborhood. Therefore, employer-based housing is needed.

113069.explanation: Allocating municipal budget for a fireworks show is not our priority.

113076.explanation: The (major) maintenance of school buildings is carried out based on a schedule. This also includes new construction. So yes, money will be spent on the improvement of school buildings, but only according to when this maintenance is necessary. And perhaps that will only be after this period.

113080.explanation: This situation already exists. Children in primary school can also use the school library.

113081.explanation: The police must focus on enforcement of criminal offenses, so that the public order officers (boa's) can concentrate on areas where supervision is really needed. This is about deploying the boa's more effectively.

113083.explanation: It is not an objective in itself to establish a women's shelter in Druten.

113084.explanation: Many residents from our villages want to continue living there. Due to the dual aging population, it is additionally important that young people can stay living in their own municipality.

113086.explanation: There are no large parks with holiday homes in our municipality. So this is not an issue.

113087.explanation: Infill development within the cores is preferred.

113089.explanation: This continues the current policy to support people with low incomes.

113090.explanation: The current arrangement is already generous.

113092.explanation: This comes at the expense of the already limited parking space in the neighborhoods.

113093.explanation: This is a good measure in terms of traffic safety.

113094.explanation: A caregiver acts not from their faith, but from their profession.

113097.explanation: Dog owners pay for facilities for their dogs, such as off-leash areas and dog waste bins.

113098.explanation: Tourists should contribute to the recreational facilities in our municipality.

113099.explanation: Businesses are important for employment in the municipality of Druten.

113101.explanation: As long as there is so much demand for housing for our own residents, unlimited priority for people with a residence permit is no longer sustainable.

113106.explanation: There are plenty of other subjects that are more important for our children to learn.

113108.explanation: With an appropriate height in the right location.

113110.explanation: In neighborhoods where parking pressure is high, solutions need to be considered.

113112.explanation: According to current public health standards, we see no room for wind turbines.

113115.explanation: Construction of an extra lane, median strip or physical separation, and a safe traffic situation at the gas station in Afferden.

113116.explanation: Our municipality already has enough beautiful natural areas such as the Afferdense and Deestse Waarden.

113057.explanation: We want a municipality where the rights of all people are protected. Every form of discrimination is unacceptable, so in principle there should be an equal number of women and men at every workplace. The quality of the people remains decisive.

113059.explanation: We can agree with this statement. We do want to note, however, that this must not come at the expense of quality. In any case, we believe that village community centers should be owned by the municipality. Management can then remain with volunteers.

113062.explanation: This is a great way to accelerate the energy transition. It is important that people with low incomes are supported more than those with high incomes. It is also important that people in rented homes have the same benefits.

113065.explanation: Those who employ these people must also arrange their housing. Municipal oversight is desired.

113069.explanation: We have always been in favor of a general fireworks ban. We think it is a great idea for the municipality to organize a New Year's Eve party, possibly including a fireworks show.

113073.explanation: The countryside must be protected. Building houses by farmers on their own land leads to rural areas becoming cluttered. We do not want that. Moreover, the municipal council recently adopted a policy: 'Protected village views and protection of cultural-historical values.'

113076.explanation: Improving school buildings can be a matter for the municipality. If the building is owned by the school board, then they are responsible. They have received funds for this. If an emergency situation arises, the municipality should step in. After all, a good building is necessary for good education.

113080.explanation: Druten needs a good library. A library in a village or a mobile library bus visiting the village is desirable. More and more elderly want to read and be assisted with digital skills. This is also a great asset for schools.

113081.explanation: Quite a few people, especially elderly, do not always feel safe. Speeding is common, youths race on scooters and fatbikes, and fireworks are still set off. More and better enforcement is needed.

113084.explanation: We can imagine that people from their own municipality or village get a higher place. But when the situation of people outside the municipality is very urgent, there is always a place for them. We absolutely do not support the slogan: 'Our own people first.'

113086.explanation: We are against permanent residence in holiday homes. This too often leads to unclear situations.

113087.explanation: New construction outside the village boundaries is not desirable. We have a policy in Druten: 'Protected village views and cultural-historical values,' which regulates this. In case of urgent housing needs, it is possible, after good consultation, to designate agricultural land as building land.

113089.explanation: We have many people living in poverty, so debt relief is necessary.

113090.explanation: Combating poverty is a high priority. Children are also, and especially, victims of it. We also support unconditional assistance to the food bank.

113092.explanation: Charging points in parking lots. Many people charge at home. When there is a shortage of charging stations, it is desirable to install charging stations in parking areas.

113093.explanation: A 30 km/h speed limit within the built-up area is a necessity. On many roads, streets, and neighborhoods, people drive too fast. Additionally, traffic calming measures are needed, such as installing pedestrian crossings. Principle: slow traffic has priority over fast traffic.

113094.explanation: Integration also means meeting people of different faiths. It is not the faith that matters, but the quality of care.

113097.explanation: We are against abolishing the dog tax. After all, the polluter must pay.

113098.explanation: We are in favor of raising this tax to the national level.

113101.explanation: Refugees with a residence permit often live in very poor conditions. We understand the pressure on the housing market, but the situation of these people is sometimes so bad that priority is desired.

113102.explanation: We must prevent dumping waste in rural areas. Therefore, disposal of waste should be free of charge.

113106.explanation: Let's first provide good lessons in the Dutch language! And thereby combat illiteracy.

113108.explanation: No high-rise buildings in the small villages. In Druten itself, this depends on the location of the high-rise.

113110.explanation: More housing is needed, people must be able to live, the car comes second.

113112.explanation: Wind turbines are necessary to protect our climate. Druten is part of the region, the Netherlands, and the world. The municipality of Druten must also meet its sustainability obligations. Currently, there are only solar parks. Land that is now completely unusable for nature! Without wind turbines, Druten will not be energy-neutral by 2040.

113115.explanation: Widening this road leads to even more traffic and thus more nuisance. We prefer speed-limiting measures that reduce traffic.

113116.explanation: Priority to nature. Nature reserves are urgently needed for, among other things, our bees. The highest priority is biodiversity.

statements.title: No wind turbines should be allowed in Druten.

titleParts.glossary: These are modern wind turbines that convert wind power into electricity and can be up to 150 meters high.

statements.title: The new college of mayor and aldermen must have an equal number of men and women.

titleParts.glossary: The college of mayor and aldermen, the municipality’s executive board.
The mayor leads the municipality and chairs the municipal council. The mayor is not elected but appointed by the national government, after recommendation by the municipal council.

titleParts.text: en wethouders moet het aantal mannen en vrouwen gelijk zijn.

statements.title: It should be possible for farmers to build houses themselves on their land.
Druten needs more municipal enforcement officers (BOAs) who focus on public safety.

titleParts.glossary: People who, for the municipality, check whether citizens and businesses comply with the rules.

titleParts.text: (boa’s) komen die zich met veiligheid bezighouden.

statements.title: The maximum speed limit in built-up areas should be set to 30 km/h.

titleParts.glossary: The maximum speed that cars and motorcycles are allowed to drive on the road.
An area with buildings (houses, other structures).

statements.title: Residents who want to generate sustainable energy together should receive financial support from the municipality.

titleParts.glossary: Energy based on resources that are effectively infinite, such as sunlight and wind.

titleParts.text: op willen wekken, moeten financiële steun krijgen van de gemeente.

statements.title: When allocating social housing, applicants from the municipality should be given priority.

titleParts.glossary: A rental home with a low rent (in 2026 at most €932.93 per month) for people with a low income.

titleParts.text: moeten woningzoekenden uit de eigen gemeente voorrang krijgen.

statements.title: The municipality should organize a fireworks show on New Year's Eve.
The municipality must continue to give refugees with a residence permit priority for social housing.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet vluchtelingen met een

titleParts.glossary: The right to reside in the Netherlands. The term can refer to the right itself or to the card that shows someone has that right.
A rental home with a low rent (in 2026 at most €932.93 per month) for people with a low income.

statements.title: Residents should be able to take over the management of, for example, a sports hall or a public green space from the municipality.
The municipality should invest money in improving all school buildings during the next council term.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet in de komende

titleParts.glossary: The municipal council is elected for a period of four years.

titleParts.text: geld steken in het verbeteren van alle schoolgebouwen.

statements.title: The municipality should stop forgiving municipal taxes for low-income residents.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet stoppen met het

titleParts.glossary: That people no longer have to pay for something.
Taxes you have to pay to the municipality (e.g., sewer charge, waste disposal charge, dog tax).

titleParts.text: voor inwoners met een laag inkomen.

statements.title: The municipality of Druten should designate more protected natural areas.
The municipality should prevent new construction outside existing village boundaries.
The municipality should designate more parking spaces as charging points for electric cars.
There should be one larger library in Druten instead of smaller libraries in each village.
The municipality should abolish the dog tax.

titleParts.glossary: Tax paid to the municipality for owning one or more dogs.

statements.title: The municipality should raise the income threshold for receiving poverty assistance.
More business parks should be developed in Druten.
People who need care should always be able to choose a caregiver who matches their religion, culture, or beliefs.

titleParts.text: Mensen die zorg nodig hebben, moeten altijd kunnen kiezen voor een zorgverlener die past bij hun geloof, cultuur of

titleParts.glossary: What someone believes about life, such as a religion or personal conviction.

statements.title: A women's shelter (domestic violence refuge) should be established in Druten.

titleParts.glossary: Shelter for women who experience violence from their partner or family. The shelter is secured; sometimes the location is secret.

statements.title: There should be more parking spaces in the neighborhoods.
The municipality must allow holiday homes to be used as a permanent residence.
The tourist tax should be increased.

titleParts.glossary: The tax you must pay when you stay overnight in a hotel or at a campsite.

statements.title: The municipality should make it possible for all primary schools to pay attention to the Maas and Waal dialect.
Employers in agriculture and horticulture should provide housing for seasonal workers on their own premises.

titleParts.text: Werkgevers in de land- en tuinbouw moeten zelf zorgen voor de huisvesting van

titleParts.glossary: People who do work that only takes place during certain seasons, often in agriculture and fruit picking.

statements.title: More high-rise residential buildings may be allowed in Druten.
Dropping off waste at the recycling center should be free of charge.

titleParts.text: Het inleveren van afval bij de

titleParts.glossary: A place where citizens and businesses can bring different kinds of waste.

statements.title: The municipality should ask the province to widen the Maas and Waal road (N322) to four lanes.
